activity-log.description: Capability set for the activity-log (Recent Changes) secondary window built in commands/activity.rs and routed to ActivityLog.svelte in main.ts. Needs event IPC for the activity:list / activity:append stream plus close + set-focus. ActivityLog's <header> is a Tauri drag region (data-tauri-drag-region), so the window MUST also grant core:window:allow-start-dragging — without it the webview's start_dragging invoke is denied by the ACL and surfaces as an UnhandledRejection (Sentry HQ-SYNC-WEB-3). Supersedes the retired notification-history capability (the separate notification-history window was retired in favor of the inline NotificationFeed + this standalone Recent Changes window).

default.description: Default capability set for the HQ Sync menubar webview.

desktop-alt-capability.description: Capability set for the Indigo-only desktop alternate UX window. The Meetings page self-fetches calendar data via the app's meetings_* commands directly (meetings_list_upcoming, meetings_list_scheduled_bots, meetings_list_accounts, meetings_list_calendars_for_account, meetings_list_memberships) — same as the standalone meetings-window. The Projects surface self-fetches local-PRD data via get_local_projects and get_local_project_prd (US-003, local HQ-tree reader with vault fallback), plus get_local_project_readme (US-009, reads a project's sibling README.md by its prd path with the same path-traversal guard) and get_local_company_goals (reads a company's objectives + initiatives from companies/<slug>/board.json with the same slug/path guard; missing board → empty goals), and persists status changes via set_local_project_status (US-010, atomic + round-trip-validated write to companies/*/board.json) and set_local_story_passes (US-010, atomic toggle of a story's passes in the project's prd.json) — both reuse the same is_within HQ-folder path guard and the Indigo gate. The Accounts (CRM) surface self-fetches the vault-synced read model via get_company_crm_projection (hq-native-crm US-010, local-first read of companies/<slug>/crm-projection.json with the same slug/path guard; missing/unsynced → JSON null) and falls back to get_company_crm_projection_vault (vault-API read of /companies/<uid>/crm-projection; not-provisioned/404 → JSON null, auth failures propagate) — the same local-first + vault-API fallback pattern the Board surface uses, with no network to Attio/Stripe/PandaDoc/Neon. The Library surface self-fetches local skills + workers via get_library_root and get_library_company (read-only scans of core/workers/registry.yaml plus .claude/skills, personal/skills, and companies/<slug>/skills SKILL.md frontmatter), with detail reads get_library_worker_detail (reads a worker's worker.yaml by its registry path) and get_library_skill_detail (reads a SKILL.md's frontmatter + markdown body) — both reuse the same is_within HQ-folder path guard and the Indigo gate. The Files surface self-fetches the local file tree via get_company_file_tree (US-001, eager per-company walk) and get_company_file_content (file preview, MAX_FILE_BYTES cap), plus list_hq_dir (US-010, LAZY single-level listing of an HQ-relative directory — empty path = HQ root — for on-expand loading of the root tree without a full eager walk); all reuse the same is_within HQ-folder path guard, the curated dev-noise filter, and the Indigo gate. These are app-registered Tauri commands authorized by core:default (custom commands are not gated by per-command permission identifiers in this ACL, only plugins are), so no allow-* tokens exist or are added for them.

dm-banner.description: Capability set for the dm-banner custom notification window. Receives the DM payload via IPC (core:event) and invokes banner_window_ready (ready-handshake show), banner_action (re-emit notification:dm-action → App.svelte opens detail / copies prompt), and dismiss_banner (auto-timeout / close). Without this file the banner's invoke()/listen calls silently fail the capability check and the window (created visible:false, shown only by the handshake) never appears. Mirrors dm-detail.json.

dm-detail.description: Capability set for the dm-detail secondary window. Receives the DM event via IPC (core:event) and invokes the dm_detail_window_ready handshake + show/focus. Without this file the window's invoke()/listen calls silently fail the capability check, so dm_detail_window_ready never reaches Rust and the window (created visible:false, shown only by the handshake) never appears — the symptom is clicking 'Open details' on a DM notification doing nothing. The window reuses the shared <Conversation/>, so it also invokes the shared messaging commands fetch_dm_thread + send_dm and, for US-025 Reactions UI, toggle_reaction (POST add / DELETE remove /v1/notify/reactions) + fetch_reactions (GET /v1/notify/reactions) + set_active_conversation (registers which conversation + message ids are open so the SINGLE DM poll path can re-fetch reactions on a 'reaction' wake); the reaction realtime event message:reaction is emitted by that poll path and authorized by core:event:default. All custom commands are authorized by core:default (custom commands are not gated by per-command permission identifiers — only plugins are). Mirrors share-detail.json / messages.json.

drift-detail.description: Capability set for the drift-detail secondary window. Needs event IPC to receive the DriftReport from Rust + permission to invoke the handshake / restore commands. Without this file, invoke() calls from the window silently fail at the capability check — the symptom is a window that stays stuck on its loading state because drift_window_ready never reaches Rust.

meeting-permissions.description: Capability set for the meeting-permissions wizard window. Self-fetches state via `meetings_permissions_state` and deep-links to System Settings panes via `permissions_open_settings` — no event-handshake needed.

meetings-window.description: Capability set for the Upcoming Meetings detail window. Needs to invoke meetings_* commands directly (self-fetches data, no main-window handshake).

messages.description: Capability set for the dedicated Messages window (US-009). The shell self-fetches its data via app-registered Tauri commands authorized by core:default (custom commands are not gated by per-command permission identifiers in this ACL — only plugins are), so no allow-* tokens are added for them. The window invokes: messages_window_ready (ready-handshake show/focus), open_messages_window (focus from within), list_contacts, list_company_members, get_unread_summary, plus the shared messaging commands fetch_dm_thread + send_dm (the conversation pane reuses the DM thread + composer) and send_dm_to_email (US-010 New Message compose — POSTs /v1/notify/dm by email or personUid and returns the delivered vs connection-requested discriminant), and the connection-request commands list_dm_requests + respond_dm_request (US-011 Requests segment — list_dm_requests reads GET /v1/notify/connections/requests for the pending-request cards, respond_dm_request POSTs /v1/notify/connections/{accept|decline|block} with {pairKey} and emits dm:request-update on success). US-018 Channels segment adds the channel commands list_channels (GET /v1/notify/channels), fetch_channel (GET /v1/notify/channels/{id}/messages), create_channel (POST /v1/notify/channels), join_channel (POST /v1/notify/channels/{id}/members, self), invite_to_channel (POST /v1/notify/channels/{id}/members, {personUids}), send_channel_message (POST /v1/notify/channels/{id}/messages), list_channel_members (GET /v1/notify/channels/{id}/members), remove_channel_member (DELETE /v1/notify/channels/{id}/members/{personUid}), and mark_channel_read (POST /v1/notify/channels/{id}/read); the channel realtime events channel:new-message + channel:updated are emitted by the SINGLE DM poll path and authorized by core:event:default. US-022 Threads UI adds the thread commands fetch_thread (GET /v1/notify/threads?rootEventId=&scope=dm|channel[&channelId=|&withPersonUid=] — pinned root + reply list + replyCount for the right-side ThreadPanel), send_thread_reply (POST /v1/notify/dm or /v1/notify/channels/{id}/messages with an added rootEventId to post a reply), and set_active_thread (registers/clears which thread the ThreadPanel currently has open so the SINGLE poll path knows which thread to re-fetch on a 'thread' wake); the thread realtime event thread:new-reply is emitted by that SINGLE DM poll path and authorized by core:event:default. US-025 Reactions UI adds the reaction commands toggle_reaction (POST add / DELETE remove /v1/notify/reactions with {messageScope, messageId, emoji}) and fetch_reactions (GET /v1/notify/reactions?messageScope=&messageId= → [{emoji, count, reactedByMe}]), plus set_active_conversation (registers/clears which conversation + visible message ids the open Conversation pane shows, so the SINGLE poll path knows which messages to re-fetch reactions for on a 'reaction' wake); the reaction realtime event message:reaction is emitted by that SINGLE DM poll path and authorized by core:event:default. The compose recipient picker (RecipientPicker.svelte) additionally invokes meetings_list_memberships (to enumerate the caller's companies for per-company member groups). All HTTP to /v1/notify/* happens in Rust, not the webview, so no http: scope is needed here. Capability drift (a command registered in main.rs but missing from this window's capability file) is the #1 failure mode — every command callable from this window must remain listed in the description above and authorized by core:default below. Mirrors desktop-alt.json / dm-detail.json. Share integration: open_messages_window now accepts an optional deep-link target (stashed in PendingMessagesTarget and delivered via the messages:open-conversation event on ready/focus); the shell additionally invokes set_watched_shares + toggle_reaction/fetch_reactions with share:{eventId} scopes for the inline share-card bubbles merged into DM threads, fetch_notification_history (shares feed the timeline + rail previews), and open_claude_code_link (share card 'Open in Claude').

new-files-detail.description: Capability set for the new-files detail window. Receives the file-list payload via event IPC and invokes the ready handshake before the window is shown.

share-detail.description: Capability set for the share-detail window. Receives event list via IPC; uses window.open() (Web API, no shell plugin needed) for the HQ Console link. Share reactions + reply: the window additionally invokes toggle_reaction / fetch_reactions (messageScope 'share:{eventId}', messageId = eventId), set_watched_shares (registers the visible share eventIds with the SINGLE DM poll path so a 'reaction' wake re-fetches and emits message:reaction), and open_messages_window (the 'Message the sharer' action deep-links the Messages window with a pending conversation target).

widget.description: Capability set for the always-on-top HQ wordmark widget window. Transparent, non-activating, appearance-reactive floating mark (US-002). Receives future queued-count updates via core:event (US-003). No invoke commands in this story — core:default + core:event:default cover the webview shell.
